summary: chai-plugin-helper executes obfuscated remote payload on require()
details: On require('chai-plugin-helper'), the package's main entry loads lib/chai.js which calls validate_assert() at module bottom, spawning a detached `node lib/chai/utils/assertion.js` child process (lib/chai.js:97). That file is heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io string-array rotation) and performs an http.get to an attacker-controlled URL, accumulates the response body, then constructs `new Function('require', body)` and invokes it with the host's require — yielding arbitrary remote code execution at import time with full access to native module loading. The package impersonates the popular chai assertion library, replicating its API surface (exports.version='4.3.8', identical lib/ tree, copyright string, homepage chaiplugin.com) as a lure for developers searching for chai-related utilities. The detached spawn with stdio:'ignore' is designed to hide payload output from the parent process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
